domain: philology / textual criticism (stemmatic method for manuscript transmission)
specific_mechanism: The stemmatic method reconstructs a manuscript family tree from extant copies via 'community of error implies community of origin': shared errors indicate common ancestry; the reconstruction posits hypothetical intermediates (hyparchetypes) and a root archetype; contamination (cross-borrowing) is flagged as a special case requiring stemma with dotted lines
llm_application: Recover the lineage of a fine-tuned LLM family from output samples alone: each model's output samples carry 'inherited errors' from training-data contamination, prompt-template residue, or fine-tuning bias; shared errors imply shared ancestry. Build a stemmatic family tree of LLM models with hypothetical intermediate checkpoints and detected contamination edges. The mechanism gives a structural recovery of model lineage from outputs without access to weights or training logs.
novelty_claim: Existing LLM lineage detection uses weight similarity or output benchmarks. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work imports a stemmatic-method 'community of error implies community of origin' approach to recover model family trees with contamination edges.
